turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — what can I help you with today?
Something in my kitchen just won't heat up anymore.
Let's pin down which appliance — is it your oven or range that isn't heating, or something else?
It's my oven — a GE Profile electric range.
Got it — a GE Profile electric range oven that isn't heating. When did it start, and is there any error code on the display?
The oven won't get past warm, since last night. It shows F2 on the display.

F2 on a GE range usually means the control board read an over-temperature or sensor fault. Try a full reset first: switch the range off at the breaker for two minutes, then restore power and set a bake temperature. If F2 comes right back, the oven temperature sensor likely needs replacing.
Got it, I'll give that a shot.
Great — if the F2 returns after the reset, call back and we'll line up a technician for the sensor.

symptoms.description: oven will not heat past warm

steps_given: Reset the range by switching it off at the breaker for two minutes, then restore power and set a bake temperature.
If F2 returns, plan to replace the oven temperature sensor.
